creator: kristinacors
caption: Where the A listers stay for Met Gala Monday: Part 2 #luxuryhotels #wheretostayinnyc #metgalamonday #bestnychotels #themarkhotel

transcription.text: Let's talk about where the A-listers stay for the Met Gala. Met Gala Monday is upon us and this hotel is basically the unofficial headquarters of Met Gala Monday. Welcome to the Mark Hotel. Located on the Upper East Side in this stunning 1927 landmark building, this is the place to see and be seen before the red carpet. Everyone from Anna Wintour to Tate McRae to Maluma stays here on Met Gala Monday. According to the Director of Marketing for the Mark, 95% of guests here are Met Gala talent, while the other 5% are extremely loyal guests of the Mark that know they're signing up for the chaos and honestly love it. Now let's get into the crown jewel, the penthouse. This is the largest penthouse suite in North America. It's 12,000 square feet and starts at $75,000 a night. It also features 26-foot tall ceilings and has five bedrooms, six bathrooms, a private terrace overlooking Central Park, and even 24-hour access to Bergdorf Goodman. Apparently, rooms and suites at the Mark are typically booked by brands, and they often use these suites in so many different ways. Some brands will literally book out these suites, strip every single piece of furniture out, and turn them into full-on sewing ateliers, glam rooms, even entire production studios. And then overnight, everything gets put back like nothing ever happened. They will literally flip the room in less than 12 hours, and they'll help guests check in the next day with no idea what went down the night before. Now the lobby, that's considered ground zero on Met Gala Monday. According to the director of marketing for the Mark, it's easily covered with up to 2,000 people and filled with glam squads, photographers, videographers, and talent. The elevators are shut down completely 30 minutes prior to the guest departure, and staff at the Mark Hotel know down to the minute every VIP's arrival time to the main red carpet, and they send the elevator up according to their departure time. So everything runs flawlessly. The Mark Hotel even manages the paparazzi. Only 40 to 50 pre-approved paparazzi are invited, and they keep them barricaded on both sides of the red carpet. And the wildest part? It takes the hotel an entire year to prepare for this one night. So if you want a front row seat to the Met Gala, you don't start at the Met, you start at the Mark. And I left the link in my bio if you want to book a room.
